---
title: "State Management"
---

## Store Architecture

NanoMeme uses **Zustand v5** for state management — two focused stores with clear boundaries:

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    MS[memeStore] --> G[Generation State]
    MS --> H[History]
    SS[settingsStore] --> R[Resolution]
    SS --> A[Aspect Ratio]
    SS --> O[Auto Overlay]
    SK[useSecureStorage] --> K[API Key in SecureStore]
```

| Store | Scope | Persistence |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| `memeStore` | Generation workflow + history | In-memory |
| `settingsStore` | User preferences | In-memory |
| `useSecureStorage` | API key | `expo-secure-store` (encrypted) |

## Meme Store

The primary store manages the generation lifecycle and meme history:

```typescript
interface MemeState {
  isGenerating: boolean;
  currentPrompt: string;
  overlayText: string;
  currentImageUri: string | null;
  lastError: string | null;
  history: MemeHistoryItem[];

  setPrompt: (prompt: string) => void;
  setOverlayText: (text: string) => void;
  startGeneration: () => void;
  completeGeneration: (imageUri: string) => void;
  failGeneration: (error: string) => void;
  toggleFavorite: (id: string) => void;
  deleteFromHistory: (id: string) => void;
  reset: () => void;
}
```

### Generation Lifecycle

```{mermaid}
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Idle
    Idle --> Generating : startGeneration()
    Generating --> Result : completeGeneration(uri)
    Generating --> Idle : failGeneration(error)
    Result --> Idle : reset()
```

On successful generation, a `MemeHistoryItem` is prepended to the history array:

```typescript
completeGeneration: (imageUri) => {
  const { currentPrompt, overlayText, history } = get();
  const item: MemeHistoryItem = {
    id: String(nextId++),
    prompt: currentPrompt,
    overlayText,
    imageUri,
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    isFavorite: false,
  };
  set({
    isGenerating: false,
    currentImageUri: imageUri,
    history: [item, ...history],
  });
},
```

### History Item

```typescript
interface MemeHistoryItem {
  id: string;
  prompt: string;
  overlayText: string;
  imageUri: string;
  createdAt: string;     // ISO 8601
  isFavorite: boolean;
}
```

## Settings Store

Simple preference store with sensible defaults:

```typescript
const initialState = {
  defaultResolution: "1K" as Resolution,
  defaultAspectRatio: "1:1" as AspectRatio,
  autoOverlayText: true,
};

export const useSettingsStore = create<SettingsState>()((set) => ({
  ...initialState,
  setDefaultResolution: (resolution) =>
    set({ defaultResolution: resolution }),
  setDefaultAspectRatio: (ratio) =>
    set({ defaultAspectRatio: ratio }),
  setAutoOverlayText: (value) =>
    set({ autoOverlayText: value }),
  reset: () => set(initialState),
}));
```

## Secure Storage

API key handling uses `expo-secure-store` for hardware-backed encryption:

```typescript
import * as SecureStore from "expo-secure-store";

const API_KEY_STORAGE_KEY = "gemini-api-key";

export async function getApiKey(): Promise<string | null> {
  return SecureStore.getItemAsync(API_KEY_STORAGE_KEY);
}

export async function setApiKey(key: string): Promise<void> {
  await SecureStore.setItemAsync(API_KEY_STORAGE_KEY, key);
}

export async function deleteApiKey(): Promise<void> {
  await SecureStore.deleteItemAsync(API_KEY_STORAGE_KEY);
}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Why not AsyncStorage?
`expo-secure-store` encrypts data using the device's hardware security module (Keychain on iOS, Keystore on Android). API keys should never be stored in plain text via AsyncStorage or MMKV.
:::

## Design Decisions

### Zustand v5 (not Redux, not Context)

| Criterion | Zustand | Redux Toolkit | React Context |
|-----------|---------|---------------|---------------|
| Boilerplate | Minimal | Moderate | None |
| Performance | Selective re-renders | Needs `useSelector` | Re-renders entire tree |
| Bundle size | ~2KB | ~30KB | 0 (built-in) |
| DevTools | Optional middleware | Built-in | None |
| Persistence | Middleware | Middleware | Manual |

### Two Stores (not one)

Separation prevents unnecessary re-renders. Changing resolution doesn't cause the home screen's generation UI to re-render, because they subscribe to different stores.
